forecasts.reasoning: As of the June 6, 2025 knowledge cutoff, John Morgan had not formally announced his candidacy for Florida governor despite months of teasing the possibility. Multiple sources from mid-to-late May 2025 confirmed he had "not formally announced" and was only "considering" a run. As of June 5, 2025, David Jolly's announcement still referred to Morgan as someone to build a coalition "that John Morgan can believe in" - treating him as an uncommitted observer rather than a fellow candidate. Morgan has a history of signaling interest in political office without following through. With only ~25 days remaining until the July 1 resolution deadline after the knowledge cutoff, and the critical 365-day party affiliation deadline (June 12, 2025) having just passed without announcement, the probability of a surprise announcement is low. However, I assign 15% rather than lower because Morgan has been actively laying groundwork for a new political party and could potentially expedite an announcement if he decided to move forward.

Based on evidence available up to June 6, 2025: (1) No cancellation announcement has been made by either party as of late May 2025; (2) Bulgaria faces a prohibitive BGN 4.2 billion ($2.3B) termination cost that Energy Minister Stankov said must be paid to cancel; (3) Turkish officials have shown no willingness to cancel; (4) Bulgarian MPs have publicly stated the agreement "cannot be terminated by anybody"; (5) Bulgaria is pursuing renegotiation, not termination; (6) With only ~25 days remaining until July 1, 2025 and the extreme financial barriers to cancellation, the probability of an announced cancellation is low but non-zero given Bulgargaz's financial distress and suspended payments.

Based on evidence available through June 2025: (1) Sudan had NOT ratified AfCFTA as of April 2025 - confirmed by multiple sources (Africa International Trade, Globalex, tralac) that list Sudan among the 6-7 countries that signed but have not ratified. (2) Sudan has been consistently on the non-ratifying list since at least 2023. (3) Sudan is currently experiencing severe civil war, with government functions severely disrupted - the AU is focused on peace mediation efforts rather than trade agreements. (4) From my knowledge cutoff (June 6, 2025) to the resolution date (July 1, 2025) there are only about 3 weeks, making it extremely difficult to complete the ratification process even if political will existed. While there was some stakeholder work on a Draft National AfCFTA Implementation Strategy, this is preparatory work, not ratification itself. Given these factors, the probability of ratification before July 1, 2025 is low.
